transcript.clean_text: last day in the high mountains around andorra before the rest of the tour of catalonia becomes rolling and indeed peaks and troughs around barcelona well at the high ground of the day previous stage 4 was 166.7 kilometers destination and just like stage 3 there were three category 1 tests along the way the profile was always going to draw a strong breakaway we didn't think it would be quite as strong as it turned out to be mark solaire got himself up the road for ue team emirates who'd missed out the day previous they usually starting the day second place behind o'connor while o'connor's men were doing the chasing as this group including amaral from ragu parma lopez from trek harada from coffee dis carthy from ef education mark donovan dsm and biscara from oscar tell who taken the combativity award the day previously 24 kilometers to go and all of a sudden a selection bruna amaral of group armor going off with mark donovan donovan couldn't quite keep tabs on the man from group armor and indeed a very selective group out of the pelican with so much climbing it was always going to be a battle of attrition so who would reach out that was the big question from the pack well it was o'connor himself in the leaders green stripes jersey that went for it drawing out a response as well from colombia's igita meanwhile at the back the pace just too much from valverde who's clearly off color like so many have been george bennett then just set about tagging amaral out front and a good old drive likewise from ineos grenadiers who are selling a pace designed to grind down the opposition with sivikov uh just stalling in this race along with kryokowski and richie port a select group i think you might say from which richard carapaz was standing tall for india's grenadiers he went for it carrying his golden bike up the road well certainly a wheel to be chased i think it's fair to say and it was the colombian champion of gita who went straight onto his wheel with 2.7 to go quintana had always been within the pack he'd slowly but surely burnt out his teammates as well never putting his nose in the wind surely his turn would come and when we got down whittled finally to a thinning out group up front that did not include o'connor he was being challenged and a uso in the young riders jersey as well it was not to be his day almeida for ue team emirates took the step forward he as you can see with quintana and higita the colombian champion and it was they that would hit the gravel section right at the very top quintana looked good but was a little bit fearful of leaning it in too deeply onto the gravel surface and it was a surface that carried almeida to the line three up sprint for it had to be a photo but uet membranes took the day he moves into the young riders jersey quintana on points countback actually takes the lead remarkable tied on time well paul's another strong finish from him the karapas group coming home with johansen also on board spectacular performance we had to wait a while for the brave o'connor to cross the line he really had fought in defense of his jersey but it wasn't to be of four australians in a row a maida took the day ahead of two colombians the portuguese loud and proud same time as quintana on the line and same time overall once the modifications were factored in so it had to go to count back on points so quintana leads this race but exactly the same time as almeida who wears the young riders jersey the battle rages on and every second counts don't forget quintana has been up for intermediate sprints during this race he'll be looking for those points again to make a big one the race goes on stage five to villanova is tomorrow 206.3 kilometers the longest stage of this year's race will there be drama of course there will be a category three test out of the back door then rolling terrain with two late sprints all hell could break loose as we head back to the coast it's a remarkable race with a very level playing field and so many riders can win it we just don't know who you'll have to tune in to find out you
